name: Gödel Sentence Becomes a Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The paraconsistent kernel enriched over the Belnap FOUR lattice, which contains the B state dialetheia without triggering the principle of explosion.

VINIT.chosen_element: The uninterpreted formal system before the construction of self referential syntax.
VINIT.justification: Represents the void state prior to any naming or self reference.

TANCH.chosen_element: The paraconsistent kernel boundary that admits the B state as a designated value without collapsing into triviality.
TANCH.justification: Serves as the terminal anchor containing the entire logical space.

AFWD.chosen_element: The forward application of the incompleteness operator generating the gap between semantic truth and syntactic provability.
AFWD.justification: Acts as the forward morphism driving the system toward evaluation.

AREV.chosen_element: The descent into the paraconsistent substrate where the unprovable branch is accepted as a structural fact rather than a failure.
AREV.justification: Functions as the reverse morphism handling the negative evaluation branch.

CLINK.chosen_element: The composition of the iterated incompleteness operator satisfying the fixed point condition.
CLINK.justification: Chains the operations to verify the Frobenius closure identity.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: The self referential fixed point where the Gödel sentence encodes its own unprovability.
IMSCRIB.justification: Establishes the identity of the system through self recognition.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: The decomposition of the Gödel sentence into its semantic truth value and its syntactic provability status.
FSPLIT.justification: Forks the single statement into two distinct evaluation branches.

FFUSE.chosen_element: The reintegration of the truth and unprovability branches into the unified B state dialetheia.
FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the original input exactly as a closed logical state.

EVALT.chosen_element: The affirmative evaluation confirming the sentence holds in the standard model of arithmetic.
EVALT.justification: Anchors the positive branch with a true verdict.

EVALF.chosen_element: The negative evaluation confirming the sentence lacks a formal derivation within the system.
EVALF.justification: Anchors the negative branch with a false verdict.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The paradice where the sentence is simultaneously true and unprovable, held as a stable attractor.
ENGAGR.justification: Enters the B state where both truth values are held without resolution.

IFIX.chosen_element: The permanent fixation of the B state as a structural theorem, closing the incompleteness gap.
IFIX.justification: Records the final closed state as an append only logical fact.

phase_2.fuse_result: The Gödel sentence as a closed B state dialetheia

phase_3.void_description: The formal system prior to the introduction of self referential encoding, containing no Gödel sentence.
phase_3.true_description: The semantic evaluation where the Gödel sentence is recognized as holding in the standard model.
phase_3.false_description: The syntactic evaluation where the Gödel sentence is recognized as lacking a formal derivation.
phase_3.both_description: The B state dialetheia where the sentence is simultaneously true and unprovable, saturating the incompleteness gap.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the formal system in the void before self referential syntax is constructed.
Construct the self referential Gödel sentence that encodes its own unprovability.
Establish the paraconsistent kernel as the boundary containing the system without explosion.
Decompose the Gödel sentence into its semantic truth branch and its syntactic provability branch.
Apply the forward morphism of truth evaluation along the T arm.
Affirm the semantic truth of the sentence in the standard model, anchoring the T arm.
Apply the reverse morphism of unprovability descent along the F arm.
Affirm the syntactic unprovability of the sentence in the formal system, anchoring the F arm.
Reintegrate the truth and unprovability branches into the unified B state dialetheia.
Enter the paradice where the sentence is simultaneously true and unprovable.
Compose the iterated incompleteness operator to verify the Frobenius closure condition.
Fix the B state as a permanent structural theorem, closing the incompleteness gap.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The paraconsistent Lean kernel that blocks the principle of explosion and admits the B state.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The Frobenius round trip morphism that transports structural verdicts across the truth lattice.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The topological winding number that records the exact count of Frobenius verified closure cycles.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The Lawvere Tierney closure operator that drives the system to the B state fixed point.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Frobenius exact ZFC foundation that serves as the canonical reference for self verifying truth.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Zero entropy consumption because the topological invariant is read from the boundary without erasing information.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The formal system with an open incompleteness gap oscillating between truth and unprovability.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The formal system saturated at the B state fixed point with the gap permanently closed.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 because the cycle is a topological invariant and the B state is a stable attractor that preserves all information without erasure.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → TANCH → FSPLIT → AFWD → EVALT → AREV → EVALF → FFUSE → ENGAGR → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: Gödel Sentence Becomes a Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑱  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑳 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def g_del_sentence_becomes_a_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the formal system in the void before self referential syntax is co...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Construct the self referential Gödel sentence that encodes its own unprovabil...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [2] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Establish the paraconsistent kernel as the boundary containing the system wit...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Decompose the Gödel sentence into its semantic truth branch and its syntactic provability branch.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Apply the forward morphism of truth evaluation along the T arm.)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirm the semantic truth of the sentence in the standard model, anchoring th...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Apply the reverse morphism of unprovability descent along the F arm.)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Affirm the syntactic unprovability of the sentence in the formal system, anch...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reintegrate the truth and unprovability branches into the unified B state dialetheia.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Enter the paradice where the sentence is simultaneously true and unprovable.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [10] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the iterated incompleteness operator to verify the Frobenius closure ...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Fix the B state as a permanent structural theorem, closing the incompleteness...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def g_del_sentence_becomes_a_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (g_del_sentence_becomes_a_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def g_del_sentence_becomes_a_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (g_del_sentence_becomes_a_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF
